---
title: "Research in Finance"
subtitle: "Institute of Strategic Management and Finance, Ulm University"
listing:
  - id: lectures
    contents: lectures/*/slides.qmd
    type: table
    fields: [week, date, title]
    field-display-names:
      week: "Week"
      date: "Date"
      title: "Session"
    sort: "date asc"
    filter-ui: false
    page-size: 30
---

**Term:** {{< meta semester >}}

## About this course

*Research in Finance* equips students with the practical toolkit and conceptual grounding needed to conduct empirical finance research. Across four lectures and a final Brown Bag Seminar, students learn to:

- handle financial data in **R** (data import, cleansing, merging, transformation, visualization);
- run **statistical analyses** (correlations, parametric and non-parametric tests, linear regression with fixed effects, clustered standard errors);
- write up empirical results in **LaTeX / Overleaf** with publication-ready tables (`stargazer`) and figures (`ggplot2`);
- read and **referee** a finance research paper.

The course is project-based: a group problem set replicates a complete empirical workflow on CFTC futures data; an individual referee report critiques a doctoral presentation at the Institute's Brown Bag Seminar.

## Instructors

- **Prof. Dr. Andre Guettler** — Director of the Institute · Helmholtzstraße 22, Room 205 · [andre.guettler@uni-ulm.de](mailto:andre.guettler@uni-ulm.de)
- **Oliver Padmaperuma** — Doctoral Candidate · Helmholtzstraße 22, Room 203 · [oliver.padmaperuma@uni-ulm.de](mailto:oliver.padmaperuma@uni-ulm.de)

## Quick links

- [Syllabus](syllabus.qmd)
- [Assignment I — Problem Set](assignments/assignment-1-problem-set.qmd) ({{< meta assignment-1-problem-set-weight >}}% · due {{< meta assignment-1-problem-set-deadline >}})
- [Assignment II — Referee Report](assignments/assignment-2-referee-report.qmd) ({{< meta assignment-2-referee-report-weight >}}% · due {{< meta assignment-2-referee-report-deadline >}})
- [Gradebook (staff only)](gradebook.qmd)

## Schedule

::: {#lectures}
:::

## Registration

You **must register for "exam" {{< meta exam-id >}}** in `{{< meta registration-system >}}` by **{{< meta registration-deadline >}}**. Without this registration you cannot submit the problem set or referee report. Registered students who fail to submit will receive a fail grade (5.0).

## How students access materials

All materials are linked from the course [Moodle page](https://moodle.uni-ulm.de/). The links above are for staff convenience.
